h1: GKInstall Generated Scripts: CLI Parameter Reference

ac:rich-text-body.p: This document is the authoritative reference for all command-line interface (CLI) parameters available in the installation scripts generated by the GK Install Builder. It covers both the Windows PowerShell script (GKInstall.ps1) and the Linux Bash script (GKInstall.sh), as well as the store-initialization scripts that are invoked internally as part of the installation flow.

p: Every installation package produced by GK Install Builder contains two primary entry-point scripts:

tr.td: Main installer. Handles component installation, version resolution, onboarding, store-initialization, and launcher launch.
Functionally identical to the PowerShell counterpart for Linux environments.
Sub-script called by GKInstall.ps1 to register the workstation and store structure in the cloud backend via REST API.

ac:rich-text-body.p: Operator note: Parameters passed to GKInstall.ps1 / GKInstall.sh that are relevant to store-initialization (namely --SystemNameOverride, --WorkstationNameOverride, and --StructureUniqueNameOverride) are automatically forwarded when the store-initialization sub-script is invoked. Operators do not need to call store-initialization directly.

p: Before examining individual parameters, it is essential to understand how the scripts determine the Store ID and Workstation ID. The scripts implement a five-tier priority system. CLI parameters occupy the highest priority tier and, when supplied, cause all lower-priority detection methods to be skipped entirely.

tr.td: Values are accepted without question. If both are supplied, all remaining detection tiers are bypassed completely. If only one is supplied, detection for the missing value continues at Priority 1 and below.

When an existing station.properties file is found in the installation directory, the script enters update mode. Store ID, Workstation ID, tenant ID, and Config Service URL are read directly from that file. The CLI can still override these values at Priority 0.

When configured, regular-expression patterns are applied to the machine hostname to extract the Store ID and Workstation ID. Both two-group (e.g. STORE-101) and three-group (e.g. ENV-STORE-101) patterns are supported.
When hostname detection fails or is disabled, the script searches for component-specific .station files (e.g. POS.station) in the working directory and reads values from them.
If all automated detection methods fail, the script prompts the operator at the console to enter the Store ID and Workstation ID manually.

ac:rich-text-body.p: Update mode detection: The presence of station.properties inside the installation directory (e.g. C:\gkretail\station.properties on Windows or /usr/local/gkretail/station.properties on Linux) is the definitive signal that an existing installation is present. No explicit flag is required — the script detects this automatically on every run. Update mode suppresses environment selection prompts and re-uses the environment that was originally used to perform the initial installation, as recorded in station.properties.

tr.td: Selects which retail component to install.
Overrides all automatic Store ID detection (Priority 0).
Overrides all automatic Workstation ID detection (Priority 0).
Pins the component version, bypassing API/config-service version resolution.
Overrides the system-type name sent to the Store Manager API during registration.
Overrides the workstation name used when creating a new workstation record.
Forces a specific structureUniqueName into the structure-node creation request.
Overrides the SSL certificate password baked into the generated script.
Appends an RCS Service URL to the installation token file, or triggers autodetection.
Skips deploying installer override XML files to the installation directory.
Forces the override.check-alive flag to true in the override XML.
Forces the override.start-application flag to true in the override XML.
Runs the installer using locally bundled packages; no network downloads.
Selects a named cloud environment by its configured alias.
Prints all configured environments and exits without installing anything.
POS, ONEX, ONEX-POS, WDM, FLOW-SERVICE, LPA, LPA-SERVICE, SH, STOREHUB-SERVICE, RCS, RCS-SERVICE
ONEX is normalised to ONEX-POS; LPA to LPA-SERVICE; SH to STOREHUB-SERVICE; RCS to RCS-SERVICE

p: Selects which GK retail component the installer should handle. This value drives every subsequent decision in the script: which version to resolve, which installer JAR to locate, which .station file to create or update, which launcher template to use, and which installer override XML to deploy.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Linux — Install WDM using short alias
Linux — Install StoreHub using short alias

tr.td: Priority 0 (highest) — overrides update mode, hostname detection, file detection, and manual input

p: Provides the Store ID directly, bypassing all automatic detection mechanisms. When supplied together with --workstationId, the entire detection pipeline (hostname regex, .station file scan, interactive prompt) is skipped. When supplied alone, the workstation ID is still resolved through the normal priority cascade starting at Priority 1.
If a value was also detected automatically (via hostname or file), the script will log an explicit override notice of the form CLI Override: Using Store ID '<cli>' (detected: '<detected>').

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Override store ID for automated deployment
Linux — Provide store ID using alternative casing

p: Provides the Workstation ID directly, bypassing all automatic detection mechanisms. Semantically equivalent to -storeId but for the workstation component. When used with -storeId, both values are locked at Priority 0 and no further detection runs. When used alone, the Store ID is still resolved through the priority cascade.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Fully automated POS install with both IDs supplied
Linux — Fully automated install, zero prompts

tr.td: Highest — applied after API/config-service version resolution, unconditionally overwriting the resolved value

p: Pins the component version to be installed to the exact string supplied, regardless of what the Employee Hub Service API or Config Service returns. The version string may include or omit a leading v — both v5.27.1 and 5.27.1 are accepted. This override is applied at Priority 0, after version resolution has already occurred, and a log message of the form CLI Override: Using version '<supplied>' (resolved: '<api-resolved>') is emitted so the override is always visible in the log file.
Use cases include pinning a hotfix version during a staged rollout, reinstalling a known-good version after a regression, or testing a release candidate before it is promoted as the official latest.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Pin to a specific POS version
bash Linux — Version override without leading v ./GKInstall.sh --ComponentType WDM --VersionOverride 5.27.1 --storeId 9001 --workstationId 1

p: Overrides the system name value that is sent to the Store Manager API during workstation registration. Normally, this value is derived from an internal map that correlates each ComponentType to a pre-configured system type string baked into the generated script at build time (e.g. the template token ${pos_system_type}). Supplying this override replaces that mapped value with the exact string provided.

This parameter is relevant in migration scenarios — most notably when migrating a workstation from one system type to another (e.g. from GKR-FAT to ONEX-CLOUD) where the cloud-side record must be updated to reflect the new system type identity.
The override is forwarded transparently by GKInstall to store-initialization during the sub-script invocation.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Register workstation under a custom system name
Linux — FAT to OneX Cloud migration override

p: Overrides the human-readable workstation name (station name) that is used when the store-initialization sub-script creates a new workstation record via the Store Manager API. Without this override, the name is derived from a default naming convention encoded in the store-initialization template (typically a combination of store number and workstation ID). With this override, the exact string supplied is used as the name of the new workstation node.

This is useful when workstation names must follow site-specific naming conventions that differ from the generator's defaults, for example A319_OneXPOS1 where the naming scheme encodes physical rack and terminal positions.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Create workstation with site-specific name

p: Injects a specific structureUniqueName value into the JSON payload sent to the Store Manager API when creating a new structure node for the workstation. The structureUniqueName is an internal identifier used by the GK cloud platform to uniquely identify a node within the organisational hierarchy. Normally this value is either derived from an existing API response (when the workstation already exists in the system) or left absent (when creating a brand-new workstation and allowing the platform to generate it).

Supplying this override is necessary in migration scenarios where the cloud-side record already exists under a known unique name, or when a specific naming scheme must be preserved for downstream systems that reference workstation nodes by their structure unique name.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Force a specific structure unique name during workstation creation
Linux — Structure unique name override for migration

tr.td: Conditional — required for WDM component; optional for others
1. -SslPassword / --SslPassword parameter; 2. SSL_PASSWORD environment variable; 3. Default value embedded at script generation time

p: Supplies the password for the SSL/TLS certificate (.p12 / PKCS#12 format) used by the installed component. When not supplied, the script checks the SSL_PASSWORD environment variable, and then falls back to the default password baked into the script during generation (the @DEFAULT_SSL_PASSWORD@ template token).
For the WDM component, a non-empty SSL password is mandatory; the script will exit with an error if no password can be resolved via any of the three mechanisms.
Providing this parameter at the command line is the recommended approach for automated deployments where the SSL certificate password must not be stored inside the script file.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Explicit SSL password for WDM
Linux — SSL password via environment variable (preferred for automation)

ac:rich-text-body.p: Providing passwords directly on the command line may cause them to appear in shell history files (e.g. ~/.bash_history on Linux) and in process listings visible to other users. For production deployments, prefer the SSL_PASSWORD environment variable and ensure it is set in a secure manner (e.g. via a secrets manager or deployment pipeline secret injection).

tr.td: String (URL or the literal string autodetect)

p: Specifies the URL of the GK RCS (Retail Content Service) instance from which the component installer will download installation packages at runtime. When provided, the RCS URL is appended to the installationtoken.txt file as the line rcs.url=<url>, which the launcher reads to know where to fetch installation artefacts.

The special value autodetect instructs the script to attempt to discover the RCS URL automatically by reading an existing rcs.url property from the component's properties file on disk. If autodetection fails (e.g. on a fresh installation with no existing properties), the operator is prompted to confirm whether to continue without an RCS URL.
Omitting this parameter entirely means the installation token will not contain an RCS URL, and the launcher will rely on packages that were pre-staged locally or obtained through another mechanism.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Linux — Autodetect RCS URL from existing installation

tr.td: No — switch (absent = deploy overrides; present = skip)

p: When specified, the script does not copy the component's installer override XML file to the installation directory. Normally, the script deploys a component-specific override file (e.g. installer_overrides.pos.xml) from the helper/ subdirectory into <install_dir>/installer/overrides/installer_overrides.xml. These override files pre-configure certain installer behaviours (such as check-alive and start-application) for the specific deployment.
Passing --noOverrides is useful when:

ul.li: The target system already has a manually configured override file that must not be replaced.
Performing a repair or version-only update where behavioural configuration should remain unchanged.
Debugging installer behaviour without the pre-configured overrides interfering.

tr.td: Mutually usable with --skipStartApplication; incompatible with --noOverrides (has no effect when overrides are not deployed)

p: After the installer override XML file is deployed, the script patches the deployed XML in-place, setting the override.check-alive property value from false to true. In the GK installer framework, setting override.check-alive to true causes the check-alive phase to be skipped, which is useful when the component being installed has a known-unavailable check-alive endpoint or when the installation must not block waiting for a health check response.
This parameter modifies the XML that has already been written to disk; it does not affect the source override file in helper/.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Linux — Skip both check-alive and application start

p: After the installer override XML file is deployed, the script patches the deployed XML in-place, setting the override.start-application property value from false to true. In the GK installer framework, setting override.start-application to true causes the automatic application-start phase to be skipped. This is useful when the component must be installed but not immediately started — for example, when further configuration is required before the application is started for the first time, or when performing a staged rollout where the start will be triggered later by an operations team.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Install without auto-starting the application

p: Switches the installer to offline mode. In offline mode, the script does not attempt to download installation packages from the network. Instead, it expects the required installer JAR and any UI package to be present locally in the working directory (typically bundled as part of an offline package created by the GK Install Builder's offline package creator). The script will locate these local files and copy them to the expected temporary directory before invoking the launcher.

This mode is necessary in environments where the installation host has no direct access to the internet or to the GK cloud platform at the time of installation, for example in air-gapped store networks, or during network outage recovery scenarios.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Offline install from pre-bundled package

tr.td: No — when absent, environment is selected interactively or from station file
-Env (primary), -EnvironmentName (accepts full environment name for fuzzy matching)

p: Selects a specific cloud environment from the list of environments configured in the generated script. In a multi-environment deployment (e.g. a package generated with DEV, QA, and PROD environments), this parameter bypasses the interactive environment-selection prompt and immediately applies the configuration for the matched environment (base URL, tenant ID, credentials).

The value supplied must match an environment alias exactly as configured in the GK Install Builder (e.g. P, DEV, Q-001). In PowerShell, the alternative parameter -EnvironmentName accepts a partial name for fuzzy matching against the environment's full display name.
This parameter is silently ignored in update mode — when station.properties exists, the environment is determined from the existing installation's recorded configuration, not from this parameter.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Non-interactive environment selection using alias
Windows — Environment selection using full name (fuzzy match)

p: When specified, the script reads the environments embedded in the script, prints a formatted list of all configured environments (alias, name, base URL), and then exits immediately without performing any installation. No credentials are consumed, no files are modified, and no network calls are made.

This parameter is primarily a convenience for operators who need to identify the correct environment alias to pass to -Env / --env without having to inspect the script source or the Install Builder configuration.

The store-initialization scripts are normally called automatically by the main GKInstall scripts. However, they can also be invoked directly for troubleshooting or for re-running the store-registration step after the main installation has completed. When called directly, all four required parameters must be supplied explicitly.

ac:rich-text-body.p: The store-initialization script requires a valid helper/tokens/access_token.txt file to be present. This file is written by the onboarding step that runs as part of the main GKInstall flow. If store-initialization is run standalone, the onboarding script must have been executed first.

tr.td: The component type string (e.g. POS, ONEX-POS, WDM). Used to look up the system name mapping for the Store Manager API.
The base URL of the cloud environment (hostname only, without scheme, e.g. mystore.cloud4retail.co).
The resolved Store ID to register. This is passed through from the GKInstall script and reflects the Priority 0 override value if one was supplied.
The resolved Workstation ID to register.
Specifies the component version to be recorded during store initialisation. When not provided, the version resolved by the main GKInstall script is used.
See section 4.5. Overrides the system name in the Store Manager API call. Automatically forwarded from GKInstall when present.
See section 4.6. Overrides the workstation name for new workstation creation. Automatically forwarded from GKInstall when present.
See section 4.7. Injects a specific structureUniqueName into the create-structure API request. Automatically forwarded from GKInstall when present.

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Run store-initialization standalone for a migration
Linux — Run store-initialization standalone for a migration

h2: 6. Update Mode and the station.properties File
p: A critical behaviour of the GKInstall scripts is the automatic detection of update mode. This section describes that behaviour in detail because it directly affects how several CLI parameters are interpreted.
At startup, the script checks for the presence of a station.properties file inside the component installation directory:

If the file exists, the script enters update mode. If the file does not exist (or the installation directory does not exist at all), the script performs a full installation. There is no explicit CLI flag to force or suppress this behaviour.

tr.td: Read from station.storeId and station.workstationId entries in station.properties (Priority 1). CLI overrides at Priority 0 still take precedence.
Read from configService.url and station.tenantId entries in station.properties. Used for environment matching.
The interactive environment-selection prompt is suppressed. The script attempts to match the stored base URL against the configured environments.
Hostname detection, file detection, and manual input are skipped.
CLI parameters at Priority 0 (--storeId, --workstationId, --VersionOverride) still apply even in update mode, overriding the values read from station.properties.

h3: 6.3 Forcing a Specific Store or Workstation ID During an Update
p: If the station.properties file contains incorrect values, or if the script is being used to move a workstation to a different store or workstation number, CLI parameters can be used to override the values read from the file:

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — Update but override the store ID from station.properties
ac:structured-macro.ac:plain-text-body: # station.properties says storeId=9001, but we need to re-register to store 9002 .\GKInstall.ps1 -ComponentType POS -storeId 9002 -WorkstationId 1

p: For CI/CD pipelines and silent deployments where no operator is present, supply all required values on the command line to eliminate every interactive prompt:
h3: 7.2 PVH-Style System Type Migration (FAT to OneX Cloud)
p: When migrating an existing FAT workstation to the OneX Cloud system type, the system name in the cloud backend must be updated. The following combination of overrides handles this:

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Windows — FAT to OneX Cloud migration with all overrides
Windows — Offline install with known store/workstation IDs

h3: 7.4 Staged Update: Install Without Starting Application

ac:structured-macro.ac:parameter: Linux — Install update, skip check-alive and auto-start for staged activation
Windows — WDM installation with explicit RCS URL

p: The following table summarises how parameters interact with each other and with the update-mode detection logic.

tr.td: Entire detection pipeline is bypassed. No hostname regex, no .station file scan, no interactive prompt.
Store ID locked at Priority 0. Workstation ID still resolved via Priority 1–4.
CLI value wins. station.properties value for station.storeId is overridden.
Replaces the version resolved from API/Config-Service after resolution completes. Log shows both the resolved and the overridden value.
--skipCheckAlive has no effect because no override XML file is deployed. --noOverrides takes precedence.
Both XML properties are patched in the same deployed override file. Both phases are skipped.
Forwarded automatically to store-initialization sub-call. No need to pass it to store-initialization directly.
Silently ignored. Environment is determined from station.properties.
All other parameters are irrelevant — script prints environments and exits immediately.
Supported. Script copies local installer JAR/UI package for the update run.
CLI parameter takes precedence over the environment variable.

p: The Bash version of GKInstall accepts multiple casings for several parameters. The following table documents all accepted variants.

ac:rich-text-body.p: PowerShell is natively case-insensitive for parameter names. All parameter names in GKInstall.ps1 and store-initialization.ps1 can be supplied in any casing. The table in this section applies only to the Bash scripts.

tr.td: Configuration file written to the installation directory after a successful installation. Contains the Store ID, Workstation ID, Tenant ID, and Config Service URL for the installed component. Its presence is the trigger for update mode.

A short identifier for the GK retail software component being installed (e.g. POS, WDM, FLOW-SERVICE). Drives installer selection, version resolution, launcher template choice, and override XML selection.

An internal identifier assigned by the GK cloud platform to a node in the organisational structure hierarchy (store, workstation). Used by the Store Manager API to uniquely identify workstation nodes.

A per-component XML configuration file (e.g. installer_overrides.pos.xml) that pre-configures the GK installer's behaviour, including check-alive and start-application directives. Deployed to <install_dir>/installer/overrides/.
A phase in the GK installer lifecycle that verifies the installed component responds to a health-check request before considering the installation complete.
A phase in the GK installer lifecycle that automatically starts the installed component service or application at the end of the installation process.

A GK service that serves installation packages to endpoints within the store network. When an RCS URL is provided in the installation token, the launcher fetches artefacts from the local RCS rather than directly from the cloud.

A short label (e.g. PROD, DEV, Q-001) assigned to a cloud environment in the GK Install Builder configuration. Used to select the correct base URL, tenant ID, and credentials when multiple environments are embedded in the same installation package.
The ordered hierarchy by which the GKInstall scripts determine Store ID and Workstation ID. CLI parameters have Priority 0 (highest); manual user input has Priority 4 (lowest).
